title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week brought major improvements to system reliability and data quality checks. OpsPal now automatically detects and prevents common Salesforce configuration errors that could break your workflows, while also cleaning up outdated settings and connections behind the scenes. The platform also added stronger validation rules to ensure your automation runs smoothly without interruption.

entries.headline: Added stronger validation rules for automation workflows
details: Ensures all workflow steps produce valid results
Prevents advisory workflows from blocking your operations
Speeds up workflow testing with irrelevant commands

entries.headline: Automatic system cleanup keeps OpsPal running smoothly
details: Removes outdated cache files older than 7 days
Cleans up stale session files older than 5 minutes
Alerts you when memory usage approaches limits

entries.headline: System automatically removes broken connections and outdated settings
details: Works across Windows, Linux, and Mac systems
Detects and removes any marketplace connections
Cleans up settings for all connected platforms

entries.headline: Prevents common Salesforce configuration errors that break workflows
details: Blocks field additions when Dynamic Forms are active to prevent invisible fields
Automatically assigns record types to prevent permission errors
Converts blocking workflow errors to warnings to keep operations running

entries.headline: Automatically installs missing dependencies and reports installation issues
details: Captures detailed installation logs for troubleshooting
Provides manual fix commands when automatic installation fails
Runs background fixes for safe dependency issues

entries.headline: Eliminated false missing package errors during startup
